content.claim: Hungary exhibits significantly higher socioeconomic achievement gaps (23.1% of variance attributable to family background versus OECD average of 14.8%), lower rates of top performers (9.3% versus 12.6% OECD average), and higher rates of low performers (28.1% versus 23.0% OECD average).
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Cross-sectional correlations do not establish that tracking structure causes these gaps; confounding factors (funding, teacher quality, immigration demographics) are not isolated.
Temporal trend data needed to assess whether these gaps have widened or narrowed alongside tracking policy.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
